programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps two women whose friendship is being put to the test by a Bengal cat. Plus, a feline with a nasty streak causes concern for a mother-to-be

programme.title: Made by Machine: When AI Met the Archive
programme.desc: Hannah Fry and a virtual host present a new way of making television as the BBCs Research and Development uses Artificial intelligence to delve into the treasures of the BBC Archive. Computers trawl through more than quarter of a million shows using a variety of machine-learning techniques, and are let loose to create new mini BBC Four-style programmes-within-a-programme

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor discovers how carving up the landscape in order to build canals helped further mans understanding of the earth below. William Smith, a surveyor on the Somerset Coal Canal, discovered a way of ordering layers of rocks and eventually created the first geological map of England and Wales. His ideas about the age and formation of the earth were developed long before Darwin published his theories of evolution

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.sub-title: Weatherman Walking: Dylans Walks - Swansea and Llansteffan
programme.desc: In the first of two special programmes originally shown to celebrate the centenary of Dylan Thomass birth, Derek Brockway strolls around the poets home town of Swansea. Accompanied by actor Adrian Metcalfe, Derek sets off from Dylans childhood home on Cwmdonkin Drive and walks through the citys parks and down to the seafront, before visiting some of the writers haunts in later life. He then heads west to the Llansteffan peninsula where his guide, artist Osi Rhys Osmond, leads him through the countryside that was home to many of Dylans relatives. They include his aunts farm, which would provide the inspiration for the poem Fernhill

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Exploring the artificial intelligence required to replace human drivers, focusing on issues including the ethics of driverless car crashes and the impact on jobs. Despite the glossy PR and positives such as reduced exhaust emissions, the reality is that the future of driverless cars might not be all good news

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the early canal builders who struggled with the rugged terrain of Englands Pennine hills. Creating a network of canals in this landscape was an major challenge, but connecting the powerhouses of Yorkshire and Lancashire was a great incentive at the time of the Industrial Revolution. Liz considers the strategic considerations and implications of this massive engineering project

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.sub-title: Weatherman Walking: Dylans Walks - New Quay and Laugharne
programme.desc: Derek Brockway presents the second of two specials originally shown to celebrate the centenary of Dylan Thomass birth. First he travels to the seaside town of New Quay on Cardigan Bay, where the poet and writer lived with wife Caitlin during the Second World War. Jeff Towns, chairman of the Dylan Thomas Society, joins Derek to explain how Thomas was inspired to write some of his greatest work during this era. For his final walk, to Laugharne, the presenter is accompanied by Thomass grandaughter Hannah Ellis, who reveals the significance of the town to both her grandparents and her family today

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Plants
programme.desc: Part two of two. Exploring how technical developments are enabling people to see the world of plants in greater detail, allowing insight into their remarkable abilities to adapt and survive

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The story of a giant otter cub rescued and raised by Brazilian naturalist Carolina Vargas. The creature has to be bottle fed, learn how to hunt fish and be taught how to survive the predators of the worlds largest wetland, the Pantanal in South America

programme.title: Tribe
programme.desc: Bruce Parry treks into the jungles of Sarawak, Borneo, in search of the last nomadic Penan, a tribe of hunter-gatherers whose forest home is in the process of being cut down around them. Their traditional way of life is about to disappear

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.sub-title: Bernard Hill Remembers - Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: The late actor Bernard Hill, who famously played the character of Yosser Hughes, reflects on the acclaimed drama about life in recession-hit Britain during the Thatcher era. He looks back on how he managed to get the role, why it struck such a chord with the public and what he hopes contemporary audiences will take away from the series

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Alan Bleasdales influential Liverpool-set drama, originally shown in 1982. Chrissie and his friends sign on at the benefit office before heading to a construction site for some cash-in-hand work - but investigators are on their trail. However, mistakes are made on both sides, with tragic consequences. Starring Michael Angelis, Bernard Hill and Alan Igbon

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Dixie continues to sign on the dole while working nights as a security guard on the docks - and when a Department of Employment investigator calls, his wife is convinced they are being spied on. Meanwhile, the former foreman comes under pressure to take bribes for allowing goods to be smuggled off ships on his watch. Second part of Alan Bleasdales drama, starring Tom Georgeson

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Chrissie and his wife Angie are driven to despair over their financial problems, to say nothing of their constant hounding from the Department of Employment investigators. Starring Michael Angelis, Julie Walters and Jean Boht

programme.title: Tribe
programme.desc: Bruce Parry treks into the jungles of Sarawak, Borneo, in search of the last nomadic Penan, a tribe of hunter-gatherers whose forest home is in the process of being cut down around them. Their traditional way of life is about to disappear

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the early canal builders who struggled with the rugged terrain of Englands Pennine hills. Creating a network of canals in this landscape was an major challenge, but connecting the powerhouses of Yorkshire and Lancashire was a great incentive at the time of the Industrial Revolution. Liz considers the strategic considerations and implications of this massive engineering project

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.sub-title: Weatherman Walking: Dylans Walks - New Quay and Laugharne
programme.desc: Derek Brockway presents the second of two specials originally shown to celebrate the centenary of Dylan Thomass birth. First he travels to the seaside town of New Quay on Cardigan Bay, where the poet and writer lived with wife Caitlin during the Second World War. Jeff Towns, chairman of the Dylan Thomas Society, joins Derek to explain how Thomas was inspired to write some of his greatest work during this era. For his final walk, to Laugharne, the presenter is accompanied by Thomass grandaughter Hannah Ellis, who reveals the significance of the town to both her grandparents and her family today

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor recounts the story of canal mania - a boom period of frenzied activity that helped develop Britains modern financial economy, now centred in London. The new canal capitalists made money by investing and speculating in waterways used to carry fuel and goods around the country. However, not all canals were profitable and investors soon discovered that such a venture created losers as well as winners

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on an eight-mile trip along the Caledonian Canal, the waterway that cuts through beautiful Scottish mountain country and is regarded as one of the most ambitious canals of its time. Its engineer, Thomas Telford, intended not only to create an engineering marvel, but also provide badly needed jobs and wealth for the Highlands

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett travels from Britain to Jerusalem via Sicily, examining the expansion and unchecked ambition of the Norman empire, and how its impact still resonates in todays culture and politics. He discovers how, under the leadership of Scandinavian raider Rollo, these descendants of the Vikings first established themselves in northern France and became an unstoppable force of Christian knights and warriors

programme.title: Misery (1990)
programme.desc: A successful author finds his relief at being rescued from a near-fatal car crash is severely tempered by the discovery that his saviour is a fan with psychopathic tendencies. The situation takes a turn for the worse when she discovers he has killed off her favourite character - and sets about coercing him to do a rewrite in nightmarish fashion. Oscar-winning thriller, starring James Caan, Kathy Bates and Lauren Bacall

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.sub-title: Mark Lawson Talks to Stephen King
programme.desc: Interview with renowned author Stephen King, known for influential horror novels such as Carrie, The Shining and The Tommyknockers. His other popular works outside the genre include The Shawshank Redemption, The Green Mile and Stand By Me

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary in which Professor Jim Al-Khalili examines how some of historys greatest minds began to uncover the secrets of the universe by using and manipulating light. In the first edition, the presenter considers the significance of Greek mathematician Euclids landmark discovery that light moves in straight lines. He then moves on to Renaissance Italy, where Galileo Galilei unlocked the potential of telescopes in locating Earths true place within the cosmos, before assessing the implications of Danish astronomer Ole Roemers groundbreaking finding - that light moves at a finite speed

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines sculpture in the 20th century, an era of innovation, scandal and creativity. He examines the artistic revolution spearheaded by Eric Gill and Jacob Epstein, which paved the way for the work of Gilbert and George and Damien Hirst. He also explores the art forms rise in popularity, from First World War memorials to the landmark work of Antony Gormley and Rachel Whiteread

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor recounts the story of canal mania - a boom period of frenzied activity that helped develop Britains modern financial economy, now centred in London. The new canal capitalists made money by investing and speculating in waterways used to carry fuel and goods around the country. However, not all canals were profitable and investors soon discovered that such a venture created losers as well as winners

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on an eight-mile trip along the Caledonian Canal, the waterway that cuts through beautiful Scottish mountain country and is regarded as one of the most ambitious canals of its time. Its engineer, Thomas Telford, intended not only to create an engineering marvel, but also provide badly needed jobs and wealth for the Highlands

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett travels from Britain to Jerusalem via Sicily, examining the expansion and unchecked ambition of the Norman empire, and how its impact still resonates in todays culture and politics. He discovers how, under the leadership of Scandinavian raider Rollo, these descendants of the Vikings first established themselves in northern France and became an unstoppable force of Christian knights and warriors

programme.desc: The mathematician explores stories emerging from the world of AI, meeting individuals whose lives have been transformed for better or worse by this new technology. Hannah begins by looking at the story of teenager Jaswant Singh Chail, who in 2021 began a relationship with an AI chatbot called Sarai. James then sets the dealers the task of finding an 18th century treasure

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Yorkshire coastal village of Flamborough and discovers its historic chalk buildings. Local historian and archaeologist Andrew Jones shows Ben around Flamborough Tower. Constructed in 1674 and standing 87 feet tall, the tower was built with the purpose of improving ship to shore navigation but would ultimately be used by local law enforcers in a bid to prevent smuggling

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The team explores the Arctic Ocean, one of the most hostile environments on Earth, yet one that plays a crucial part in controlling the climate of the planet. Paul Rose and the crew dive beneath the polar ice cap to examine how the ice, the dominant feature of the ocean, is shrinking and how this could affect the rest of the world

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Film-maker Chris Terrill captures an intimate and personal portrait of life on board ultra-modern super aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth, as its crew learns the intricacies of a warship that will change the way Britain goes to war. In this first instalment, early 2016 sees sailors begin to arrive in Rosyth dockyard in Scotland where the ship is still in construction, but nearing completion. Captain Jerry Kyd and his 700 sailors are assembling to take the prototype warship to sea for the first time. These trials herald what should be 50 years of service

programme.title: Hostage

programme.desc: Fifty years ago an Australian preacher set up a religious community in New Zealand known as Gloriavale. Today it is home to 650 people who live a closeted existence away from the rest of society. This documentary examines the dark secrets at the heart of this Christian sect and the allegations that its followers are indoctrinated to an oppressive existence for life

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on what life was like for the second and third-generation members of Gloriavale, growing up in a world where every decision is made for them. New Zealand journalist Melanie Reid recounts her experience of going undercover inside the community in the 1990s, and the work she did to try to stop the abuse she discovered

programme.title: Escaping Utopia

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: Antique dealers Ian Humphries, Harry Khwaja and Jo Roberts go on a buying trip to Sommières in the South of France, looking for items to sell online for a profit. Harry haggles hard on an oversized jewellery box he thinks his American clients will fall in love with, and romance is in the air for Ian too, who takes home a large plaster cherub statue. Jo goes for a hand-built toy washstand she plans on reselling as a decorative piece to her dedicated followers

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea and finds that due to Dutch settlers in the Elizabethan era, its not as next the sea as it once was. Ben talks to historian Onyeka Nubia, who reveals that the village was part of a cosmopolitan community that introduced Britain to the canary and even the beer people drink today

programme.title: Italys Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and historian Michael Scott uncover the hidden history of three Italian cities, beginning with Naples, from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. The pair explore the impact of the volcano of Mount Vesuvius, and how it both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Alexander and Michael also delve into a labyrinth of underground spaces constructed by ruling Bourbon kings in the 19th century, that helped to sustain the city

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Kate Bateman and Adam Partridge value items in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire, including a wooden top toy and old coins that could be worth thousands of pounds. Presenter Paul Martin meets a local man who has been making violins for 40 years and tries his hand at woodwork

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt test their general knowledge skills ahead of double points Friday

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the penultimate day of the weeks quiz, and todays destination is Bristol. Paul Gorton leads the unreliable tour, dishing out facts and fibs like theyre going out of fashion. Former EastEnders star Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Apollo from Gladiators, all want to top the weekly leaderboard

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Scotland must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress a panel of exacting judges - chef Tom Kerridge, former Great British Menu Champion of Champions Lorna McNee, and comedian Phil Wang. The guest judge is Bafta-winning actress Katherine Parkinson. How will she judge dishes celebrating the British film industry? Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent Scotland at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis, Allys pride is at stake as he and son Noah prepare their ewes to take on mainland breeders at Dingwall show and market. The Glenns are hoping for good scanning results after a devastating loss of sheep in winter floods, while the pressure mounts as Lucy and Adam must negotiate an earlier completion date on the farm in Scotland, secure their finances and ensure the dairy herd is disease free

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.sub-title: The Hunt for Arlene Frasers Killer - Part Two
programme.desc: The concluding part charts a decades-long quest for justice, as Arlenes family and the police fight through courtrooms and appeals to uncover the truth about her disappearance. The police investigation is rigorously tested through two murder trials and a dramatic appeals process, with the process going from the High Court in Edinburgh in 2003 to the Scottish Parliament in 2025, where Arlenes sister Carol continued her determined campaign to uncover the truth about what happened

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the Egypt task, the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.sub-title: Derry Girls: Decades of Domestic Abuse
programme.desc: Figures show that on average one in four women living in Northern Ireland will experience domestic abuse in their lifetime. In 1976, two women armed with a bucket and a ladder broke into a building in Londonderry and opened the citys first refuge for women and children fleeing abuse. Five decades on, Foyle Womens Aid remains an ongoing place of safety. Jennifer OLeary uncovers some of their stories as Spotlight investigates a service shaped by the violence of others

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: In March 2018, a driverless car struck and killed a woman crossing the street in Tempe, Arizona, making it the worlds first pedestrian fatality from a car that was being driven by AI. But there was also a human operator behind the wheel, monitoring the vehicle, and a decision had to be made who was responsible, the human, or the AI? In her first TV interview, operator Rafaella Vasquez tells the story of the crash and its aftermath, while Hannah also talks to the lead detective on the case. She also tracks down a whistleblower from Uber, the company behind the driverless vehicle, and takes her first rides in autonomous vehicles in the US and UK

programme.title: LS Lowry: The Unheard Tapes
programme.desc: An insight into the secretive matchstick men painter as he guides viewers through his revealing life story in unheard interviews from the end of his life. Recorded by a young fan, Angela Barratt, in 1972, the tapes uncover much about Lowrys life story and the changing north he captured in his iconic paintings. Lip-synced by actors Ian McKellen and Annabel Smith.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh host the second quarter-final. Four contenders must face five brutal events before going head to head in the legendary Eliminator. Giant climbs back onto Everest, Sabre defends her territory on The Edge, and Apollo launches a rocket in the Gauntlet

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry, including venison loin smoked in gunpowder tea inspired by Skyfall and a frangipane custard tart in honour of Brave. Veteran judge Adam Handling helps to decide who deserves to say and who should go home. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Norberto Paredes reports from Venezuela on the future of its oil sector, asking whether the return of US firms is the answer the country needs. BBC Eyes forensic investigators also uncover the story of last years mass protests in Nepal that toppled the government. With eyewitness accounts, they reveal where the order to use live ammunition came from and how unarmed demonstrators were killed as authorities lost control of the crowds

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Demitchi acts like a mum to her younger sister Felicia, but its coming across as controlling. Bear sets the feuding sisters a blindfold task to show them how to work together, while psychologist Tara Quinn-Cirillo helps them to understand how their conflict might be rooted in their childhood

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: The Battle of the Sports Stars
programme.desc: Sporting legends from past road trips, football pundit Chris Kamara, wheelchair basketball star Ade Adepitan and tennis ace Judy Murray, have their eyes set on antiques glory

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: In Abeokuta, Clive tests his basketball skills on the court with an inspirational coach, before attending the birthday celebrations of a local Gospel singer to see just how Nigerians throw a party. Hell also get hands on to create authentic tie-and-dye Adire clothes and join in with an incredible contemporary dance troupe

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: In Morocco, Clive travels to Marrakesh, known around the world as the Red City for the red clay buildings that line its streets. He meets a Medina chef whose slow roasted lamb, cooked in underground ovens, has had the Royals of approval. In a souk, he masters the art of creating stunning tile mosaics, learns the skills required to make authentic Moroccan rugs, before spending the night in the High Atlas Mountains

programme.title: Bermuda Triangle: Into Cursed Waters
programme.desc: The team heads to a remote target they believe to be the long-lost Marine Sulphur Queen. This massive tanker disappeared in 1963 with a crew of 39 and is one of the most iconic missing Bermuda Triangle wrecks

programme.title: Evidence of the Unexplained
programme.desc: Thousands of UFO sightings are reported every year, along with terrifying abductions and other bizarre occurrences. This series examines these accounts and explores recently declassified top secret UFO files, casting new light on historic cases

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including Coronation Street, The Sky at Night, The Stolen Girl and The Piano. They also share opionons on Mickey Rourke on Celebrity Big Brother, savoury cocktails on Saturday Kitchen, the Katy Perrys journey into space

programme.title: Millionaire Hoarders
programme.desc: Retired artists Carole and Roger have filled every room of their nine-bed Georgian home with unusual objects theyve collected from around the world. Their country pile is in urgent need of repair; and with little income coming in, they call on the experts to help sift through the items to potentially unearth prized items that could raise life-changing sums of cash. Plus, high-end pawnbroker Rachel investigates a huge collection of jewellery owned by former lawyer Emma, who gave up her high-flying job to follow her dream of starting a jewellery business and needs extra money to stay afloat

programme.title: Secret Genius
programme.desc: Alan Carr and Susie Dent host the grand final. The four remaining contestants must place famous geniuses correctly on a giant board, before two are chosen to face the final challenge - a nail-biting second round of fluid reasoning and memory games

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay strives to turn around the fortunes of Puerto Rican restaurant El Cantito. The establishment, located in Yonkers, New York, has struggled to stay afloat post-Covid amidst the breakage of key appliances, staff problems and such unfortunate instances as uncooked chicken being served. Can Gordon turn things around?

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by comedians Jason Manford, Lou Sanders, Bella Hull and Zack Polanski for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights from the food and chat show, hosted by Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj restore a 160-year-old derelict chapel in Pwllheli, on Wales Llyn Peninsula, to turn it into a forever home with a working pottery studio for the community. In this first episode, Keith and Marj discover the local pigeons have moved in

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit their oldest-ever house-hunters, Margot and Henry from Berkshire, who were looking to move to somewhere a little more modern after 50 years in the same place. They also catch up with young couple Wendy and Simon, who were desperate to find their first home in Derby

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Bar Wars V - The Final Judgement
programme.desc: Rival bar-owner Gary backs out of the annual Halloween prank-playing due to ill health, but Sam doesnt believe he is genuinely poorly - with dire consequences. Elsewhere, stuck-up parents Frasier and Lilith dress up to take their son trick-or-treating

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Where Have All the Floorboards Gone?
programme.desc: Its Norms birthday, so as a surprise, Sam arranges for Boston Celtics basketball player Kevin McHale to drop in and present him with a team jacket. However, when the star gets dragged into a pointless discussion, it obsesses him for weeks to come - and completely destroys his ability on the court. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson and George Wendt

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam asks Carla to help him exact revenge on John Allen Hill, the irritating owner of the seafood restaurant above Cheers, but she ends up falling for him instead. Meanwhile, Cliff faces a new challenge at work _ riding a horse. Comedy, guest starring Keene Curtis, with Ted Danson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray steels himself for the delicate matter of telling Ally where babies come from - only to learn she is more interested in discussing the meaning of life itself. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra runs for school president, but Ray decides to vote against her to avoid looking after the twins during all the meetings she would have to attend. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra tries to create a sense of affection and familiarity by addressing Marie as mom - but the gesture backfires badly and offends her real mother, who finds it inappropriate

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Three Dates and a Break-Up - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Crane brothers congratulate themselves on getting rid of Sherry - but like the proverbial bad penny, she soon turns up again, determined not to let Martin go without a fight. Kelsey Grammer stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne falls out with Sherry and flees the apartment after the two have a blazing row. With nowhere else to go, she asks if she can spend the night at Niless flat, leaving him quaking with excitement. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles receives divorce papers from Maris - and to show he is not as spineless as people think, he signs them immediately. Later, though, he begins to regret his impulsiveness. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: After Niles asks him whether he should stay with Maris, Frasier wanders the streets in film-noir fashion to find an answer for his long-suffering brother. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe and the team face more challenges in the form of two vintage Harley-Davidson motorcycles they would like to buy in Pennsylvania

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Rochdale to rescue an Opel Manta GTE. The car belongs to former amateur rally driver Alan, who loved his Manta so much he couldnt bear to part with it, even when it went off the road. With Alan becoming a bit of a recluse after developing health problems and losing his wife to cancer, his family and friends hope that the Car S.O.S treatment will be just the thing to bring Alan back out of his shell

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Maidenhead, Berkshire, Simon OBrien falls in love with an old ships carpenters chest, while Henry Cole takes away a miniature tether car, which he hopes to get moving again

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Train driver Wayne and train manager Kate from Staffordshire met at work 21 years ago and its been full steam ahead ever since. Property expert Lee Juggurnauth shows them the best places their £85,000 budget can secure in the village of Pinar de Campoverde in south-east Spain

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In France, new plans and schemes keep restaurateurs Andrew and Richard on their toes and there are costly complications at Gordon and Shonas dive centre in Marbella. Best friends Janey and Sharon swap life in Blighty for a French adventure in Corrèze

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals stay the night at Barbara and Elizabeths Holly Lodge Boutique BandB in Norfolk. Grout doubts in the bathroom get things off to a bad start but the hosts dance into the good books with an afternoon soundtracked by an old Wurlitzer organ

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hotel receptionist Daffyd hosts a nutritious dinner party on the third night in south Wales. After a few kitchen mishaps during the day, the night gets off to a competitive start with Sandra challenging the host - an aspiring personal trainer - to a press-up competition

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe helps keen dancers Jane and James search for their dream holiday home on eastern Crete: a holidaymakers paradise full of stunning beaches with crystal-clear waters, archaeological sites and picturesque villages and towns. For their budget of £130,000 Craig lines up five fabulous options, from traditional Cretan houses packed full of charm and a villa with wrap-around views, to a beautiful bungalow with bags of potential

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil help Sue and Richard from Mapperley decide whether to sell or renovate their home. After Richard suffered a stroke two years ago he struggles to get around the two-storey house and thinks one-level living would be much easier for them, but Sue loves their home and thinks with a complete re-jig of the ground floor they could make it fit their needs. While Kirstie conjures up a plan to make their home more accessible, Phil sets out to find a one-floor home for them with some gorgeous views

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: The Butcher of Suburbia - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. The inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman, and the macabre lengths the killer went to in attempting to conceal her death. Annette Smiths lodger reported that she has been missing for several weeks. Missing Persons find no trace of her. Detectives from the Major Crime Unit are called in on a gripping and disturbing case that shocks detectives

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: The Butcher of Suburbia - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Detectives from the Major Crime Unit at Bedfordshire Police have a suspect in custody who admits to the crime - but the confession is only the beginning of a case that will bring a series of deeply disturbing twists and turns in a tale that shocks and surprises the experienced police team. Cameras follow as the police continue to probe the suspect and the interview becomes more and more chilling

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Andrew is rushed to Queens Medical Centre after sustaining a severe facial injury from a machine at work. Trainee advanced clinical practitioner Emily Rowe oversees the 40-year-olds challenging treatment. In Resus, doctors need to work out the cause of 61-year-old Johns sudden blackouts, which are causing him to fall over, including falling down the stairs. Tony has come to AandE after being crushed by a forklift truck which trapped his ankle and cut his head

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Fifty-three-year-old ex-Naval Pilot Rich is suffering from a 16cm tumour that stretches from his jawline down to his heart, wrapping around his carotid artery. Consultant Head and Neck Surgeon Professor Vin Paleri requires close collaboration with vascular and cardiac specialists to delicately remove the tumour, potentially sacrificing the major artery in the process

programme.title: The Dunblane Tapes
programme.desc: The story of the grassroots campaign to ban private handguns in Britain through the recently uncovered recordings of a bereaved parent after the Dunblane school shooting in 1996. After the event, three mothers who were outraged that the perpetrator could legally carry four handguns and over 1,000 rounds of ammunition, launched a nationwide campaign to lobby the government to ban the private use of handguns in Britain, gaining support from the bereaved parents of Dunblane including John Crozier, who documented the most successful grassroots campaign in British history

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay steps in to assess a troubled Brooklyn restaurant thats sinking into debt. As an additional problem, its co-owners are constantly clashing. Can the acerbic chef help sort out the finances and operations before it folds?

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: American comedian and actress Desiree Burch joins Joe Lycett for some snow-filled shenanigans in the city of Rovaniemi - the official home of Santa Claus - in Finnish Lapland on the edge of the Arctic Circle. The pair try out ice floating by donning rubber suits and climbing into a freezing lake. After they thaw out, they head for a five-course tasting menu inspired by Lappish ingredients including reindeer. On day two, Joe and Desiree experience ice fishing, ride in a traditional Sami reindeer caravan, learn some local handicrafts and look for the Northern lights

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith and Marj submit their application to transform the chapel into a home, pottery studio and events space. While they anxiously wait for the news, they turn their attentions to ripping out rot, learning some Welsh and opening the chapel to pitch their audacious plan to the local community

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp transforms fabric by dyeing it with turmeric, and embellishes an old lampshade

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer and Kirstie Allsopp catch up with Amy and Takie, who were desperate to buy their first property in London, but had to put their dream on hold until they could save enough to make buying worthwhile. There is also an update on Vicky and Andy, who combined their search for a home and two businesses in Wiltshire on a budget of £400,000 - an idea they eventually reconsidered when the reality of the market became clear

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Woody has trouble proposing to Kelly, especially as Henri has already tricked her into saying she will marry him, claiming he is about to be deported. American bar-room comedy, starring Woody Harrelson and Jackie Swanson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: When Rebecca gets the all-clear from the doctor she realises its Sam who needs to take a fertility test, while Frasier finds it difficult to make a will. Ted Danson stars, alongside Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam and Rebecca go on a baby-making weekend break but begin to have doubts about the whole thing when they realise they are not in love. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of three. Marie gives Debra the silent treatment for being rude to her, so Ray talks the pair into making amends - but as usual in the Barone household, his efforts backfire. Starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part two of three. Marie and Debras feud continues, so Ray and Frank use it to their advantage, while Robert tries to get the warring pair to make friends. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra accuses Ray of still not being able to read her moods, prompting them to reminisce fondly about the first night they spent together. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A message mistakenly left on an answering machine leads to a possible romance for Frasier, who rushes off to the airport to meet the caller. Guest starring Linda Hamilton, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A cancelled date leaves Niles, Martin and Daphne suspecting Frasiers supermodel escort is just a figment of his fevered imagination - so he tries to provide photographic evidence of the relationship. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier go gift-shopping with a vengeance as Martins 65th birthday approaches, vying with each other to be the apple of their fathers eye on his big day. Starring John Mahoney, Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. Niles throws a fancy-dress Halloween party with a literary theme as a benefit for the Library Association, which he attends dressed as Cyrano de Bergerac, while a misunderstanding lets one or two skeletons out of the closet. Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce star

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe tries to buy a set of art deco cigarette machines, and one of his and Frank Fritzs recent purchases leaves an appraiser speechless

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Dorset to pick up a Ford Escort MK2 RS2000, which belongs to carpenter Mike. After his son was involved in a horrific road accident, Mike gave up work and supported him through a four-year recovery. Now, Mikes family want to give something back to offer thanks to such a devoted father by presenting him with his fully restored RS2000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Simon OBrien are in Woking, Surrey, where they find a sidecar - to be transformed into a boat - and a water cylinder that Simon might make into a trendy lamp

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman joins the team as Clare Balding presents live coverage of Crufts 2026 from the National Exhibition Centre. The action gets off to a flying start with all the thrills and speed of Flyball, as reporter Radzi Chinyanganya and commentators Jim Rosenthal and Graham Partridge call the live heats. Plus: Claudia Winkleman gives her first impressions of the worlds greatest dog show, and special guest Ellie Simmonds returns to her home patch in Birmingham for canine chat

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In France, renovation plans give restaurateurs Andrew and Richard sleepless nights, while poor weather hits BandB hosts Janey and Sharon, who are hoping to launch a new cookery school in Corrèze. In Spain, things get too quiet at Abi and Darrens silent disco on the Costa Blanca, and Gordon and Shonas Marbella dream hangs in the balance

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks final visit is to Helen and Carls self-catering glampsite, Peacock Lake, in Nottinghamshire. The hosts feedback has ruffled plenty of feathers throughout the week, so their guests expectations are sky-high, and lavatory letdowns and smelly bedding dont make for happy glampers

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth to host in south Wales is childrens doctor Sophie, who despite usually being a vegan has decided to cook meat for her guests in an attempt to grab the grand. Sophies night is full of surprises as she reveals a number of hidden talents. Claire is flabbergasted at her job, Richard is shocked at where she went to university, and Sandra thinks she can give her a run for her money at her hidden hobby. The night isnt without incident as Sophies inexperience at cooking meat comes back to bite her and makes Daffyd uncomfortable

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is in Lower Normandy in France with Tracy and Lee from Greater Manchester, who run a dog walking business together. The couple have a budget of £70,000 to buy a holiday property that could one day become their permanent home. Lucy hunts out five properties for the pair to consider

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents all the live action as Crufts 2026 gets into full swing. And Claudia Winkleman hustles the bustle of the Birmingham NEC halls for the best features at the worlds greatest dog show. The Working Group is the first to be judged, with the winner going through to Sundays prestigious Best in Show final. This group includes guard dogs, and search and rescue breeds like Dobermanns, Rottweilers and Newfoundlands. Then its time for the Pastoral Group to take centre stage. Australian shepherds have been the most successful breed in this group in recent years. And if that wasnt enough excitement, athleticism and speed are the order of the day in the Agility and Flyball competitions, with commentary from Jim Rosenthal and Graham Partridge

programme.title: Molly vs the Machines
programme.desc: Documentary about the death of Molly Russell, the 14-year-old who in 2017 took her own life as a result of depression and negative social media content, and her father Ians fight for online safety. The film focuses on what happened to Molly in the lead-up to her tragic death and the broader economic logic behind AI and big tech as it continues to shape, influence and infiltrate peoples lives

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics views on Britains Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Staffords Rite Of Passage, Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time, BBC News (Andrew Arrested) and ITV News (Mandelson Arrested)

programme.title: Lake District Rescue
programme.desc: The team attend a man who has slipped while crossing a stream and is left with a bone sticking out of his arm. In Wasdale, an emergency call has come in for a man with a badly broken leg on the Corridor Route to Scafell Pike. Darkness is closing in, and the first challenge is to locate the casualty. Leaving him on the hill in plummeting temperatures would be life-threatening

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey to transform Maxs Bar and Grill. Even though its been serving up famous hotdogs for almost 100 years, its on the verge of crumbling, with over $1million of debt. The situation isnt helped by a power struggle between the divorced owners, who still work together

programme.title: French Exit (2020)
programme.desc: Frances Price, an ageing Manhattan socialite, cashes in the last of her possessions and resolves to live out her twilight days anonymously in a borrowed apartment in Paris, accompanied by her directionless son Malcolm and a cat named Small Frank. programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla opposes her sons decision to become a priest, Sam finds a way to get even with John Hill, and Cliff begins a new life as a stand-up comedian. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Millionaire Hoarders
programme.desc: Retired artists Carole and Roger have filled every room of their nine-bed Georgian home with unusual objects theyve collected from around the world. Their country pile is in urgent need of repair; and with little income coming in, they call on the experts to help sift through the items to potentially unearth prized items that could raise life-changing sums of cash. Plus, high-end pawnbroker Rachel investigates a huge collection of jewellery owned by former lawyer Emma, who gave up her high-flying job to follow her dream of starting a jewellery business and needs extra money to stay afloat

programme.title: Secret Genius
programme.desc: Alan Carr and Susie Dent host the grand final. The four remaining contestants must place famous geniuses correctly on a giant board, before two are chosen to face the final challenge - a nail-biting second round of fluid reasoning and memory games

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay strives to turn around the fortunes of Puerto Rican restaurant El Cantito. The establishment, located in Yonkers, New York, has struggled to stay afloat post-Covid amidst the breakage of key appliances, staff problems and such unfortunate instances as uncooked chicken being served. Can Gordon turn things around?

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by comedians Jason Manford, Lou Sanders, Bella Hull and Zack Polanski for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights from the food and chat show, hosted by Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith Brymer Jones and his partner Marj restore a 160-year-old derelict chapel in Pwllheli, on Wales Llyn Peninsula, to turn it into a forever home with a working pottery studio for the community. In this first episode, Keith and Marj discover the local pigeons have moved in

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit their oldest-ever house-hunters, Margot and Henry from Berkshire, who were looking to move to somewhere a little more modern after 50 years in the same place. They also catch up with young couple Wendy and Simon, who were desperate to find their first home in Derby

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Bar Wars V - The Final Judgement
programme.desc: Rival bar-owner Gary backs out of the annual Halloween prank-playing due to ill health, but Sam doesnt believe he is genuinely poorly - with dire consequences. Elsewhere, stuck-up parents Frasier and Lilith dress up to take their son trick-or-treating

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: Where Have All the Floorboards Gone?
programme.desc: Its Norms birthday, so as a surprise, Sam arranges for Boston Celtics basketball player Kevin McHale to drop in and present him with a team jacket. However, when the star gets dragged into a pointless discussion, it obsesses him for weeks to come - and completely destroys his ability on the court. Bar-room comedy, starring Ted Danson and George Wendt

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam asks Carla to help him exact revenge on John Allen Hill, the irritating owner of the seafood restaurant above Cheers, but she ends up falling for him instead. Meanwhile, Cliff faces a new challenge at work _ riding a horse. Comedy, guest starring Keene Curtis, with Ted Danson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray steels himself for the delicate matter of telling Ally where babies come from - only to learn she is more interested in discussing the meaning of life itself. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra runs for school president, but Ray decides to vote against her to avoid looking after the twins during all the meetings she would have to attend. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra tries to create a sense of affection and familiarity by addressing Marie as mom - but the gesture backfires badly and offends her real mother, who finds it inappropriate

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: Three Dates and a Break-Up - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Crane brothers congratulate themselves on getting rid of Sherry - but like the proverbial bad penny, she soon turns up again, determined not to let Martin go without a fight. Kelsey Grammer stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne falls out with Sherry and flees the apartment after the two have a blazing row. With nowhere else to go, she asks if she can spend the night at Niless flat, leaving him quaking with excitement. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles receives divorce papers from Maris - and to show he is not as spineless as people think, he signs them immediately. Later, though, he begins to regret his impulsiveness. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: After Niles asks him whether he should stay with Maris, Frasier wanders the streets in film-noir fashion to find an answer for his long-suffering brother. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe and the team face more challenges in the form of two vintage Harley-Davidson motorcycles they would like to buy in Pennsylvania

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Rochdale to rescue an Opel Manta GTE. The car belongs to former amateur rally driver Alan, who loved his Manta so much he couldnt bear to part with it, even when it went off the road. With Alan becoming a bit of a recluse after developing health problems and losing his wife to cancer, his family and friends hope that the Car S.O.S treatment will be just the thing to bring Alan back out of his shell

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Maidenhead, Berkshire, Simon OBrien falls in love with an old ships carpenters chest, while Henry Cole takes away a miniature tether car, which he hopes to get moving again

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game, with comedian Neil Delamere joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Train driver Wayne and train manager Kate from Staffordshire met at work 21 years ago and its been full steam ahead ever since. Property expert Lee Juggurnauth shows them the best places their £85,000 budget can secure in the village of Pinar de Campoverde in south-east Spain

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In France, new plans and schemes keep restaurateurs Andrew and Richard on their toes and there are costly complications at Gordon and Shonas dive centre in Marbella. Best friends Janey and Sharon swap life in Blighty for a French adventure in Corrèze

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals stay the night at Barbara and Elizabeths Holly Lodge Boutique BandB in Norfolk. Grout doubts in the bathroom get things off to a bad start but the hosts dance into the good books with an afternoon soundtracked by an old Wurlitzer organ

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hotel receptionist Daffyd hosts a nutritious dinner party on the third night in south Wales. After a few kitchen mishaps during the day, the night gets off to a competitive start with Sandra challenging the host - an aspiring personal trainer - to a press-up competition

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe helps keen dancers Jane and James search for their dream holiday home on eastern Crete: a holidaymakers paradise full of stunning beaches with crystal-clear waters, archaeological sites and picturesque villages and towns. For their budget of £130,000 Craig lines up five fabulous options, from traditional Cretan houses packed full of charm and a villa with wrap-around views, to a beautiful bungalow with bags of potential

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil help Sue and Richard from Mapperley decide whether to sell or renovate their home. After Richard suffered a stroke two years ago he struggles to get around the two-storey house and thinks one-level living would be much easier for them, but Sue loves their home and thinks with a complete re-jig of the ground floor they could make it fit their needs. While Kirstie conjures up a plan to make their home more accessible, Phil sets out to find a one-floor home for them with some gorgeous views

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: The Butcher of Suburbia - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. The inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman, and the macabre lengths the killer went to in attempting to conceal her death. Annette Smiths lodger reported that she has been missing for several weeks. Missing Persons find no trace of her. Detectives from the Major Crime Unit are called in on a gripping and disturbing case that shocks detectives

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: The Butcher of Suburbia - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Detectives from the Major Crime Unit at Bedfordshire Police have a suspect in custody who admits to the crime - but the confession is only the beginning of a case that will bring a series of deeply disturbing twists and turns in a tale that shocks and surprises the experienced police team. Cameras follow as the police continue to probe the suspect and the interview becomes more and more chilling

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Andrew is rushed to Queens Medical Centre after sustaining a severe facial injury from a machine at work. Trainee advanced clinical practitioner Emily Rowe oversees the 40-year-olds challenging treatment. In Resus, doctors need to work out the cause of 61-year-old Johns sudden blackouts, which are causing him to fall over, including falling down the stairs. Tony has come to AandE after being crushed by a forklift truck which trapped his ankle and cut his head

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Fifty-three-year-old ex-Naval Pilot Rich is suffering from a 16cm tumour that stretches from his jawline down to his heart, wrapping around his carotid artery. Consultant Head and Neck Surgeon Professor Vin Paleri requires close collaboration with vascular and cardiac specialists to delicately remove the tumour, potentially sacrificing the major artery in the process

programme.title: The Dunblane Tapes
programme.desc: The story of the grassroots campaign to ban private handguns in Britain through the recently uncovered recordings of a bereaved parent after the Dunblane school shooting in 1996. After the event, three mothers who were outraged that the perpetrator could legally carry four handguns and over 1,000 rounds of ammunition, launched a nationwide campaign to lobby the government to ban the private use of handguns in Britain, gaining support from the bereaved parents of Dunblane including John Crozier, who documented the most successful grassroots campaign in British history

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay steps in to assess a troubled Brooklyn restaurant thats sinking into debt. As an additional problem, its co-owners are constantly clashing. Can the acerbic chef help sort out the finances and operations before it folds?

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: American comedian and actress Desiree Burch joins Joe Lycett for some snow-filled shenanigans in the city of Rovaniemi - the official home of Santa Claus - in Finnish Lapland on the edge of the Arctic Circle. The pair try out ice floating by donning rubber suits and climbing into a freezing lake. After they thaw out, they head for a five-course tasting menu inspired by Lappish ingredients including reindeer. On day two, Joe and Desiree experience ice fishing, ride in a traditional Sami reindeer caravan, learn some local handicrafts and look for the Northern lights

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Keith and Marj submit their application to transform the chapel into a home, pottery studio and events space. While they anxiously wait for the news, they turn their attentions to ripping out rot, learning some Welsh and opening the chapel to pitch their audacious plan to the local community

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

programme.desc: Mike Wolfe tries to buy a set of art deco cigarette machines, and one of his and Frank Fritzs recent purchases leaves an appraiser speechless

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman joins the team as Clare Balding presents live coverage of Crufts 2026 from the National Exhibition Centre. The action gets off to a flying start with all the thrills and speed of Flyball, as reporter Radzi Chinyanganya and commentators Jim Rosenthal and Graham Partridge call the live heats. Plus: Claudia Winkleman gives her first impressions of the worlds greatest dog show, and special guest Ellie Simmonds returns to her home patch in Birmingham for canine chat

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In France, renovation plans give restaurateurs Andrew and Richard sleepless nights, while poor weather hits BandB hosts Janey and Sharon, who are hoping to launch a new cookery school in Corrèze. In Spain, things get too quiet at Abi and Darrens silent disco on the Costa Blanca, and Gordon and Shonas Marbella dream hangs in the balance

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks final visit is to Helen and Carls self-catering glampsite, Peacock Lake, in Nottinghamshire. The hosts feedback has ruffled plenty of feathers throughout the week, so their guests expectations are sky-high, and lavatory letdowns and smelly bedding dont make for happy glampers

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth to host in south Wales is childrens doctor Sophie, who despite usually being a vegan has decided to cook meat for her guests in an attempt to grab the grand. Sophies night is full of surprises as she reveals a number of hidden talents. Claire is flabbergasted at her job, Richard is shocked at where she went to university, and Sandra thinks she can give her a run for her money at her hidden hobby. The night isnt without incident as Sophies inexperience at cooking meat comes back to bite her and makes Daffyd uncomfortable

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is in Lower Normandy in France with Tracy and Lee from Greater Manchester, who run a dog walking business together. programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Crufts 2026
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents all the live action as Crufts 2026 gets into full swing. And Claudia Winkleman hustles the bustle of the Birmingham NEC halls for the best features at the worlds greatest dog show. The Working Group is the first to be judged, with the winner going through to Sundays prestigious Best in Show final. This group includes guard dogs, and search and rescue breeds like Dobermanns, Rottweilers and Newfoundlands. Then its time for the Pastoral Group to take centre stage. Australian shepherds have been the most successful breed in this group in recent years. And if that wasnt enough excitement, athleticism and speed are the order of the day in the Agility and Flyball competitions, with commentary from Jim Rosenthal and Graham Partridge

programme.title: Molly vs the Machines
programme.desc: Documentary about the death of Molly Russell, the 14-year-old who in 2017 took her own life as a result of depression and negative social media content, and her father Ians fight for online safety. programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

Colie Edison created every element of the Bowlero brand. Now she wants to go undercover to find out how the new Bowlero is working for staff and customers. As her alter ego Michelle, who recently lost her job as a court reporter and now wants to find a second career, Colie works as a trainee manager, a lane server, an event host and a trainee facilities manager

programme.desc: In East Anglia, three rival couples compete to host the best dinner party and win £1,000. First up are ultra-competitive poker players Kim and Martin. programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Lauras childhood friend Jane Canfield comes back to Walnut Grove. Despite her blindness, she develops a fondness for Isaiah Edwards - who is twice her age - and the two gradually fall in love

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: When a handsome called named Reverend Hale passes through Walnut Grove, he earns Reverend Aldens respect by making a generous donation to the Church, and is quickly accepted by the congregation as a strong and efficient speaker. However, he causes a stir when he begins secret meeting with the towns women secretly - especially as Almanzo and John are away on business

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Dr Marvin Haynes has been practising in Walnut Grove, but his failing eyesight prevents him from renewing his licence, leaving him angry and depressed over the loss of his career. Jenny is paralysed in an accident, but hope arrives in the form of Haynes who takes her as his last patient. Ralph Bellamy guest stars

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Ben starts to hunt animals for their skins to sell for profit. John-Boy organises a reenactment of a famous historical battle, leaving Grandpa ashamed, as he has exaggerated his own part in the actual event

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: John-Boy takes in a runaway child fleeing from her abusive stepfather. However, Grandpa is suspicious of her story

programme.title: Ballykissangel
programme.desc: A winning lottery ticket is bought in Kathleens shop, prompting the community to turn on each other in a bid to find the lucky owner. Meanwhile, Ambrose is sent on an undercover mission that could prove the making of him if he succeeds. Drama, starring Peter Hanly

programme.title: Born and Bred
programme.desc: Michael is convinced a monster is lurking in the local reservoir and persuades Eddie to try to capture the creature, and Deborahs mother arrives in Ormston unannounced. Back at the surgery, Tom is baffled when Mr Boynton comes in with a bad cough and discovers pigeons might be to blame. Medical drama, starring James Bolam and Michael French, with a guest appearance by Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Napier becomes concerned at the generally poor state of womens health in Tannochbrae, and launches a bold new campaign to set matters right. Rhona falls head over heels in love, and two surprising would-be instructors step forward to teach the locals ballroom dancing. Highland medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Rumpole of the Bailey
programme.sub-title: Rumpole and the Miscarriage of Justice
programme.desc: A mentally challenged man confesses to the murder of a respected policeman in a street riot. However, evidence surfaces that part of the confession has been altered. Judge Guthrie Featherstone presides at the subsequent trial of a detective superintendent and has to contend with Rumpole for the defence, but during this stressful time makes the unwise decision to escort legal clerk Dot Clapton to a club. Legal drama, starring Leo McKern and Peter Bowles

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Henry falls in love with Ohio University cheerleader Nancy Sue Parker, prompting Hawkeye, Trapper and Radar to take drastic measures to rescue his marriage. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda, Gary Burghoff and McLean Stevenson

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Hawkeyes desperate quest to get replacement boots sets in motion a train of deals, favours and backhanders all dependent on Klinger being sectioned and sent home - which Frank will not allow. Comedy, starring Alan Alda and Jamie Farr

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: Father Mulcahy gives his blessing when an unlisted man persuades Hawkeye to do a nose job for him - strictly against regulations - and Trapper takes the opportunity to bond with the nursing staff on the team. Comedy, starring Alan Alda and William Christopher

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: First episode of the classic comedy sequel. Bob travels down to London in his efforts to find a suitable wedding present, where fate sees him reunited with Terry, returning home after a gruelling spell in the Army. Starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam. From 1973

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Sci-fi thriller, starring Antonio Sabato Jr, Traci Lords, Matt Lasky, Chacko Vadaketh, Mitchell Gordon and Noelle Perris.

programme.title: Two Rode Together (1961)
programme.desc: The desperate relatives of people captured by Comanches many years before put pressure on the US military to bring them home. The army hires a cynical frontier marshal, who agrees to negotiate their release in return for a commission. But the townsfolk are forced to face some grim truths when he returns with just one unrecognisable captive. John Fords Western, with James Stewart, Richard Widmark and Shirley Jones

programme.title: Thunder in the Sun (1959)
programme.desc: Wagon-train scout Lon Bennett leads Basque settlers on a perilous journey to California, where they hope to establish a vineyard. However, Lons open pursuit of Gabrielle, a member of the group, causes friction - particularly for her husband and his brother. Western, starring Susan Hayward, Jeff Chandler and Blanche Yurka

programme.desc: A nurse tries to help an injured alien after his saucer crashes - despite Vincents warnings. Sci-fi drama, starring Roy Thinnes

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Part one of three. General Stockwell blackmails the gang by kidnapping Hannibal and threatening to hurt him if the trio fail to secure the release of hostages held on a plane to Spain

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An embittered woman jeopardises her life by exposing her abusive husbands drug operation. Drama, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: Starship Troopers (1997)
programme.desc: Courageous military recruits hurl themselves into a desperate war against alien insects set on destroying mankind. They blast off for the creatures home planet in a last-ditch attempt to save the human race from annihilation - little realising the carnage that awaits them. Sci-fi adventure from director Paul Verhoeven, starring Casper Van Dien, Denise Richards and Dina Meyer

programme.title: Upgrade (2018)
programme.desc: Technophobe Grey Trace is left confined to a wheelchair after being mugged. His only hope for revenge is Stem, an experimental computer chip implant that allows him to control his body. However, once implanted, Stem starts talking to Trace and takes over his body. Sci-fi thriller, starring Logan Marshall-Green and Melanie Vallejo

programme.title: Braven (2018)
programme.desc: A logging company owner and his family find themselves in a dangerous situation when they uncover a drug traffickers hidden stash of cocaine in their cabin. Action thriller, starring Jason Momoa, Garret Dillahunt and Jill Wagner

programme.title: Mojave (2015)
programme.desc: A suicidal artist drives into the desert seeking solitude, hoping to find some meaning in his shallow life. He meets a mysterious drifter who throws his life off course, and must find a way to conquer his depression in order to protect the things he cares about. Thriller, starring Oscar Isaac, Garrett Hedlund and Mark Wahlberg

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: The Invaders

programme.title: MegaFault (2009)
programme.desc: A series of devastating earthquakes are evidence of a massive crack opening up in the surface of the planet. A seismologist teams up with an explosives expert to prevent the world being completely destroyed by the quakes. Disaster movie, starring Brittany Murphy, Eriq La Salle and Bruce Davison

programme.title: Legend Lowdown
programme.desc: Take a look at all the latest movies and series coming your way this month

programme.title: The Legend of Butch and Sundance (2004)
programme.desc: The story of how two of Americas most infamous outlaws joined forces and tried to make their fortunes with a string of daring robberies. Western, starring David Rogers, Ryan Browning, Rachelle Lefevre and Michael Biehn

programme.title: The Arrival (1996)
programme.desc: A radio astronomer discovers a signal transmitting to Earth and resolves to discover its source. Travelling to Mexico, he unwittingly uncovers an alien conspiracy to take over the planet and finds his own life in danger. Sci-fi thriller, starring Charlie Sheen, Ron Silver, Lindsay Crouse and Teri Polo

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Kirk has his memory erased while investigating a mysterious obelisk on a planet inhabited by a Native American-type civilisation, and the population soon begin to believe that he is a god. Meanwhile, on board the Enterprise, Spock and McCoy try to prevent an asteroid collision that could destroy the world. William Shatner and Leonard Nimoy star

programme.title: The Invaders
programme.desc: An old friend of Vincent sees an alien in the defence plant where he works. Sci-fi drama, starring Roy Thinnes

programme.title: The A-Team
programme.desc: Part two of three. The gang is locked in a military prison awaiting trial for the murder of a colonel. Action adventure, starring Mr T and George Peppard

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A scientist who has discovered a possible cure for genetic diseases is kidnapped leading Walker to attempt to rescue him. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: Kiss of the Dragon (2001)
programme.desc: A Chinese police officer travels to Paris in search of the head of an international drug ring. However, his target is a corrupt cop who promptly frames him for murder. Fighting to clear his name, he finds an unlikely ally in an American prostitute who offers to testify against the crooked detective in return for help in rescuing her daughter. Martial arts adventure, starring Jet Li and Bridget Fonda

programme.title: War (2007)
programme.desc: FBI agent Jack Crawfords partner is murdered along with his family by a mysterious assassin named Rogue. Several years later, the elusive killer resurfaces and begins to pick off members of rival Yakuza and Triad gangs one by one, prompting the agent to set out to exact revenge. Martial arts thriller, starring Jet Li, Jason Statham, Devon Aoki, Luis Guzman and Saul Rubinek

programme.title: The Power of One
programme.desc: An English youngster is shocked by the brutal racism endemic in his South African school, and vows to fight for justice. However, an ill-judged romance with the daughter of an apartheid leader brings confusion to his life. Drama set in the 1930s, starring Stephen Dorff, Morgan Freeman, Armin Mueller-Stahl and John Gielgud

programme.title: The Reptile (1966)
programme.desc: Harry and Valerie arrive in the remote Cornish village to an unwelcoming and suspicious population. Harrys brother dies suddenly, bitten by a lethal reptilian bite. They befriend a young woman Anna whose tyrannical father controls her life, as they discover that others in the village have suffered a similar fate, their investigations lead to Anna. What they uncover is a victim of the most terrifying legacy... a destiny of mutilation and murder. Hammer horror, starring Noel Willman, Jacqueline Pearce and Jennifer Daniel.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A scientist who has discovered a possible cure for genetic diseases is kidnapped leading Walker to attempt to rescue him. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 77-year-old former motorbike racer arrives with a potentially deadly swelling of the main artery that could burst at any moment. His only option is major surgery, which could be dangerous at his age. A woman whose boyfriend was killed in a cycling accident a year previously is also injured while on her bike, and a medical technician and a mobile caterer talk about their roles in aiding people to recovery

programme.title: Saving Country Houses with Penelope Keith
programme.desc: In Chillingham in Northumberland, Britains most haunted castle reveals its secrets as four brave visitors go on a night-time ghost hunt. At Whitmore Hall in Staffordshire, the portraits get some long-overdue attention and a clean

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Patients dealt with at Kings College Hospital, London include a man who was stabbed in his own home and has injuries to his lung, arm and liver, and an elderly woman injured in a fall. A former international lawyer who lost his wife, family and home to alcoholism decides to make a change after being treated with care and compassion

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
